Welcome everyone to the longer version of the Diagnostic Sale. It is a two-page sale. Try to keep this as short and as easy as humanly possible. I think that you should print this out on double-sided paper and put them on clipboards so that the person who is actually doing the sale can literally read the questions. So the intention of this sale is to make this as scalable as possible and as easy as possible to delegate. I know that Dylan already handed this over to a salesperson. They closed like a $2400 sale in their first try who I've never sold before. So if you don't overthink this, it is a very, very, very simple process. And so you're literally just going to ask the questions. The reason I set it up this way was with the little black dots is because I think with your eyes it's easier to kind of check off the questions that you've already asked. And then you can write answers in the little gap that's underneath. And that way it looks like it's much more of an intake kind of questionnaire back and forth rather than you interrogating them or selling them. So I have the pre-meeting checklist just to make sure that any of your people who are working with you have already done the, they filled out the pre-framed questionnaire. They noted their bad habit cost section, right? Making sure that we've covered any of the costs. You're currently 205 and you want to get to either they told you the weight you're like and you're trying to get to 150. Okay, cool. So you're only going to lose 555. Great. We do this all the time. So, you know, why now? What changed? Why today? Why not yesterday? Or last week? Right? Or last month? Whatever. And so then they'll be like, well, you know, and then they may tell you that something happened, their kid said something, those are great. Let me get those ones. But it'd be like, you know, it's been a while and I've been meaning to do this and I need to do it. Right? And so then that they basically tell you the urgency, you know, so they can go to get around the, I need to think about it later. So this is all we're doing is we're overcoming the overcomes that would happen later. We're just doing it at the front of the set. All right. So what brought you today? What's your problem? What's your goal? They tell you your goal. Great. So your goals, you're trying to lose weight, tone up, gain muscle, you're currently X, you're trying to get to Y. Great. We do this all the time. Why now? What changed? Why today? Okay. Understand. So why is this important to you? Right? Well, like what is not being in shape, preventing you from doing right now that you want to do? All right. Which is the second question. And then they will tell you what they are not able to do that they wish they could do. So this is their deeper emotional feeling of what they're trying to do. And you're like, got it. Okay. I understand. And whenever you're doing each one of these, you just want to repeat back to them what they just said. They will feel like you're listening to them at a much higher degree. You'll build rapport way faster. All right. Identify the gap. So why have you been successful in the past? Right? What's been missing from the things that you've tried? All right. All of what we're trying to get them to say here is just they're missing fitness, they're missing nutrition, they're missing accountability. Which basically just means they didn't know what they were doing or they didn't stick with the thing they were doing. Right? Right. Okay. Cool. And what you can do is you can tell them the kind of story that goes along with each of them. So here it's a sustainable fitness is like, ah, you couldn't stick with the fitness part. Okay. Got it. So, you know, a lot of times when people try to do fitness, they start things and they don't like the thing, which is why they don't keep doing it. Right? Like we only do the things that we enjoy doing. Like you've never been like, man, I can't do you watch Netflix, you watch your TV shows and they'll probably say yes, because most humans do. You're like, got it. So you never think like, oh man, like I just can't get motivated to sit down and watch House of Cards. Like I just, I try to get motivated and I can't do it. But of course you don't say that because you like House of Cards and we can get exercised to be the same thing. We set to figure out which things you like the most. Does that make sense? Right. So you didn't have something that was really sustainable for you and personalized. Does that make sense? Great. Okay. If they say it was nutrition that was the issue and it might have been all three of these things. You don't know, but if, if nutrition was the issue because that's what they tried before and they couldn't stick with it, then it's like, got it. So do you think that you're going to be able to stick with chicken and broccoli for the rest of your life? Like, no, of course not. Like, okay, cool. So I mean, what we found is that people have to like the foods that they're eating. I know crazy concept for them in order to stick with it. Right. Like basically no humans eat chicken and broccoli for the rest of their life. It doesn't happen. Right. It doesn't happen. It'll definitely get you to lose weight fast, but you're not going to keep it off. Right. So what we have to do is figure out how you can hide your cake and eat it too. What I mean is have the body you want and eat cake, right. Figuratively, not too much, but like basically we'll figure out what plan of things that you like to eat that you look forward to eating and you'd be amazed at how simple we just change a couple of things out that you're already doing that'll make a massive difference. Does that make sense? Great. Okay. Accountability. So up to this point, the reason you haven't been successful is because you haven't been able to stick with it. Right. So the reason that people have the, you know, like there's three stories here you can tell any of them. You can tell an insurance story, which is like having an accountability coach is just to make sure it's the insurance. That way you know that you're going to stick with it and you fall off the insurance policy covers you and get you right back with the road. That's what it's there for. Right. And that's why the best athletes in the world still have coaches, right. And so it's the biggest lie in the world that people think that they're not going to have a coach, right. Or they think that they don't need a coach when the reality is that every single person who achieves has a coach, right. And that's why you weren't able to stick with the past, right. Because don't check in on it. Of course you won't. Right. What happens when you plateau? You don't know. Right. That's why it's good to have somebody who's already done this a hundred times. Does that make sense? Okay. Great. So ideal outcome. 12 months from now, what's the, what would make this the best decision for your time life? What would be your future pace? I tell you, really got it. Okay, Susan, I think we can help you get there in 33 weeks, right. And assuming your goal is to keep it off, it'll take another, usually just cut the time period in half. So it'll take another 16 weeks to maintain, you know, get your body to adjust. Okay. Now that's for a big, you know, that's a machine. It was a lot of weight, but if someone started to lose like 30 pounds, they're like, awesome. You know, realistically, trying to lose 30 pounds, that's going to take 15 to 20 weeks. And then probably another 10 weeks to maintain. So we're looking at 30 weeks. Okay. Just to do the whole thing. Does that make sense? Great. Okay. Do you want to hear how the program works? Yes. Okay. Great. I'll start in the gym or at home somewhere else. Whatever they say. Smart move. All right. Two, uh, second question. Do you exercise, uh, do you prefer to exercise at predetermined times or at your own convenience? Got it. Great choice. Okay. Nutritionally, do you prefer a template or a customized plan that's made for you? Good call. All right. Do you prefer to eat the same things every day or have variety? Totally makes sense. Okay. Would you prefer to look forward to it? Okay. So, um, where do you want to go from here? The whole goal is to get them to ask you the price. Okay. Got it. So, um, they're like, how much is it? Gotcha. All right. So given you came to lose, you know, 30 pounds, I think we can both agree that, and this is what we're trying to do here in this one sentence. I can guarantee the results in 30 weeks provided you respond to my messages. You meet your check-ins, which means that you're going to send me a picture of your weight every morning. All right. And you'll send me the picture of the supplements that you're going to take. All right. You take the products that I tell you to take, obviously, but otherwise you can't send me pictures of them. Um, and then you follow the sense of eating guidelines that we cited. We, we, we keep you. All right. Um, and if you don't hit it by then, I'll keep working with you until you do free of charge. You just need to make sure that you continue to take what I tell you to take from the product side. Okay. If it was 2900, then it would be 290 off. So that'd be 2,600. And then you say half of that would be 1,300. So it's like, cool. So can you do 1,300 a day instead of 2,400? All right. Great. Let's do that. And then after that, well, the only weekly payments are going to be, now you have the other 1,300 and you spread that over 30 weeks. So if you're looking at 1,300 over 30 weeks, that's where the math came from. They want 20% they do the painful discount. You're going to do times 0.8. This is going to be 2376. That's what it is, paid in full today. All right. So if you don't pay in full today, then you go back to 2,970, which is the amount that it's supposed to be. You say, cool. If you want you can pay half off, you can pay half down today and get 10% off. So then you take the 10% off the top and then you do half. Half of that number. So it's 1336. That's half. Now the other 1336 that remains, you're going to divide that over the next 30 weeks. So then it's like, cool. So that's $44 a week after that. Sound fair? And so what it is, all this does is it maximizes the upfront revenue that we're able to generate so that we can maximally acquire customers in a profitable sense. Okay. And the thing is, is you got to understand the numbers behind them if you close any of the higher ticket options. So you have less operational hazard on your side. Right. So after you go for the half down, they still can't do that. Then you're like, cool. Let's just do 599 down to get you started. And then we'll split the remainder of the duration, you know, duration time. So you're that needed by that. Right. So it's like, cool. So it'll be 599 down and then 79 bucks a week. Cool. Great. Great. Fantastic. Right. And then same thing with 299 down. And then at the end of this, if they're still like, I still can't do that. I'm out. Then you're like, cool. How about we just start you out for let's say eight weeks and then we'll go from there. Sound fair? Great. So you just close them on a shorter period of time. And then it's really just a question of like, what can you afford? I can afford a thousand bucks. Then it's like $700 split over the next 10 weeks. Right. So then it's going to be 70 bucks a week for the next 10. Okay. Great. And so after that, then you just have your simple, you know, your simple overcomes with like, I need to think about it. It's like, cool, what's your main concern? Right. Duration, me the program, you don't think it's going to, you can do it. Price like whatever it is, they'll tell you. And then you really have to paint this one. This is the hardest. This is the best clothes that I know of, which is like, let's just consider the alternatives. Right. Like you've done all these things in the past. They haven't worked for you. Right. You know that you need the accountability. Otherwise the things you're going to do, you're not going to stick with. You need the right plan. You haven't had it up to this point. So the question is like, do you want to solve this for good and be done with it and not do all these cheap things that you've done in the past? Right. Because you know that all you're going to do is come back here in six months or a year or three years, five, 10, 15, 20 pounds heavier. Right. Right. Okay. Cool. And so that's basically negative future pacing. Let's walk out the door today as though you don't sign up and see what your life looks like in a year with you gaining 20 more pounds. So you're 180 now. What if you're 200 a year from now? How much harder is it going to be then? Right. So the thing is, is we know that you need these things. Period. You need them. So like now let's look at the bad habit stuff. Let's look at how much money you're going to save. Like let's work on this. Right. As long as it's not the first thing of like, you don't believe in me. You don't believe in the program. You don't think you can do it, whatever it is. Like once you attack those things, then it's like, cool, then let's make this work. And then you're working with them to close the sale. If it's a spouse thing, do they know that you want to get in shape and fail? Yes. Are they happy that you're not succeeding? No, of course not. So do you think it would be unreasonable to think that they would support you in doing something that would make you happy? No. So you're kind of using past logic to say that they're probably going to be in support of you doing something if they know they don't like something else. Like they don't approve of you being super unhappy, right? Of course not. So then why would they disapprove of you doing something about it? Right. So that's kind of the whole point with the spouse close. And then you can also ask, what's their main concern? Because then it gets it away from them. And then usually they'll use their husband as a foil, as a dummy that they can say they have a concern. It's like, well, what's their main concern? It's like, well, I don't think they'll think that I'm going to stick with it. Okay, cool. And then you address that point rather than addressing the husband, right? And then there, if you really read, I remember, we guarantee this, like, we guarantee you that you hit it. Like when you pay the money, you're going to get there and I'm going to keep service to you until you hit it provided you do XYZ. Right? Last case you do delay pay. Price wise, it's really just, do you really want to lose this weight for money? Right? It's reestablishing the value. And then mentioning the savings that go along with this. Like right now your current lifestyle is costing you more than this well. So you'll only save money while you're doing this. So I wouldn't make money is never a concern. Right? And then you can go into vote with our dollars. We vote with our dollars with things we care about. So literally everything besides your rent, we can come. You know what I mean? Like literally everything we can figure out if this is a priority for you. Right? Because if it's not, then that's a totally different thing. But I know when you came in today, you said XYZ and I just want to make sure that is that still, is that still how you feel? And they're like, yeah, you're right. And like, okay, now that's where rapport is really important for this. Now, just as an overarching question here, whenever you get into obstacles, whenever people start, you get into the into the fist fight, you have to remember that it's not combative. You cannot change your tone. If anything, you have to relax more. And it's kind of like in a fight for like people short in their breath. They, they, they, they breathe too fast. They, they get too tense, right? When they're adrenaline spikes. So this is where practice comes into it. But the more you get into the fight, the more you get into the fight, the more you get into these sales, the more you, you have to get more childlike, more curious when you ask these questions. So, no, no way. What's your main concern? Right? Totally caring. Not like, like, well, I mean, you're going to have to do this anyways. Like, why are you, you know, like, it's not a Q and a S or you'll never close someone that way. You have to be genuinely on their side, asking questions so that they come to the conclusion that we all know is that they're just afraid to make the decision. And all we're here to do is help them make it so that we can help them. All right. So, but if someone does all of those things and takes the supplements through that period of time, the reason you do the service guarantee is because if you are serving for servicing someone past that guarantee and they're still in the supplements, you're still getting paid to service them. Okay. And so that's a key point. And most people after 30 weeks, if they've done a great job and literally had done all that stuff, it might take you an extra month to hit there. But that's going to be a very, very, very, very, very small minority of cases. And be realistic with the goals that you're setting. You know, like, be realistic. You're going to guarantee it. Be realistic. You know, give them two pounds a week. And that's it. You know, like that's, you know, that's, that's the key. You know, like that's, that's all you got to do. Okay. So, um, any who, I hope that makes sense. Um, that is the, that is the diagnostic sale, uh, the longer version. Uh, Cole, my sales manager who turned around a lot of 24s, um, made a second one or he and I worked together on a second one that's shorter. It's one page. Um, for once people kind of get this one down and really understand some of these questions. This is a script to ask the questions. It'll lead them naturally to the conclusion. And, um, with this kind of pricing, you'll be able to close way more at way higher ticket, which the entire objective of this selling script. So use wisely.
